*** 1 FATAL *** A VEHICLE ATTEMPTED TO DRIVE THROUGH FLASH FLOOD WATERS FROM A CREEK ALONG KY HIGHWAY 52 NEAR THE ESTILL AND LEE COUNTY LINE. THE VEHICLE WAS EVENTUALLY SWEPT DOWNSTREAM WITH THE DRIVER INSIDE AND NO PASSENGERS...CAUSING THE DRIVER TO DROWN.

A RESIDENTS DRIVEWAY WAS WASHED OUT AND SOME OF BACKLICK ROAD WAS UNDER WATER. THE NEARBY CREEK WAS OVERFLOWING DUE TO HEAVY RAIN RUNOFF FROM A MINE.
HEAVY RAIN CAUSED NOT ONLY FLOODING...BUT A NUMBER OF MUDSLIDES IN AND AROUND WITTENSVILLE. MULTIPLE TREES WERE ALSO WASHED OFF HILLSIDES AROUND THE AREA.

A NUMBER OF ROAD CLOSURES AND DAMAGE DUE TO FLASH FLOODING WERE REPORTED IN AND AROUND WITTENSVILLE. ROADS THAT WERE CLOSED BECAUSE OF THE FLOODING INCLUDED HWY 581, HWY 993, WHIPPORWILL ROAD, THE RIGHT FORK OF LINDY BRANCH, AND KY RT 3224. FLOODING WAS ONGOING AS OF 1035 PM...BUT THE WATER WAS BEGINNING TO RECEDE.
FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED ALONG ABBOTT CREEK NEAR PRESTONSBURG.
FLASH FLOODING WAS REPORTED IN THEALKA AND NIPPA WITH WATER ENTERING HOMES.

THE JOHNSON COUNTY 911 REPORTED THAT HOMES AND ROADS IN THE WITTENSVILLE AREA WERE BEING FLOODED. AFFECTED AREAS INCLUDE THE TUTOR KEY TO RIVER AREA. ROUTE 581 FROM THE 2600 BLOCK TO THE 3600 BLOCK HAD 2.5 FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS IT. THE WATER HAD REACHED DEPTHS OF 5 TO 6 FEET DURING THE WORST OF THE FLOODING. ROUTE 132 AND THE RIGHT FORK OF LINDY BRANCH BOTH HAD CHEST DEEP WATER ACROSS THEM WITH FOUR HOMES IN THE AREA FLOODED.
MAJOR FLOODING WAS OCCURRING IN ALLEY FORK OFF US 23 IN NORTHWEST FLOYD COUNTY.

BRIEF TOUCHDOWN OF AN EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS UP TO 80 MPH OCCURRED ON A FARM ALONG HWY 11. A SMALL FARM BUILDING WAS DESTROYED AND ANOTHER HAD WINDOWS BLOWN OUT. A FEW TREES WERE UPROOTED AS WELL. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT THE END OF A 3 MILE PATH OF INTERMITTENT STRAIGHTLINE WIND DAMAGE.

ONE HOME ON US 60E IN THE MOREHEAD AREA HAD WATER ENTER IT. AT LEAST ONE HOME WAS A TOTAL LOSS DUE TO THE FLOODING. OTHER HOMES IN THE MOREHEAD AREA WERE EITHER DAMAGED BY FLOOD WATER OR HAD WATER COME INTO CONTACT WITH THEM AT SOME POINT. THE FLOODING OCCURRED PRIMARILY ALONG HWY 60E AND CRANSTON ROAD IN THE MOREHEAD AREA. A NUMBER OF RESIDENTS WERE SEEKING ASSITANCE DUE TO THE FLOODING.
